author: nflaig
content:
<@&1417820113981145228> I am curious, does your implementation optimize all the 4 scenarios already?

- (1) 8192 if you have 8192 builder deposits (valid and for new builders), ETH can be recycled
- (2) 8192 if you have 8192 builder deposits with invalid signatures (sprinkle in some invalid ones to make batching harder), ETH will be burned for invalid sigs
- (3) fill up `pending_deposits` with invalid deposits for same pubkey (each has different invalid sig), then do 1 valid builder deposit for that pubkey, can be up to 50k or more, depending on how much ETH you can burn
- (4) 8192 if you have 1 builder deposit (valid) but 8191 validator deposits for the same pubkey (sig invalid) before in the same payload, ETH will be burned for the validator deposits

<@592004585506340885> have we tested these scenarios, or did we just test (1) and maybe (2) so far?

author: tersec
content: Nimbus hasn't particularly optimized any of this, no

author: stefanbratanov
content: Teku optimizes only for 1 so far, for 2 if there is invalid builder deposit, the whole signature batching fails and we go back to validating signature one by one, not sure how we can optimize this further with the current BLS apis, for 3 we only cache the pubkey for the valid pending deposits, so we would end up looping for that pubkey if all are invalid

author: jtraglia
content: Yes, I've tested 1 and 2 on some clients. For 3, does it matter if there's a valid builder deposit at the end? The "then do 1 valid builder deposit for that pubkey" part. For 4, do you expect validator deposits to the verified differently here? Good test ideas though.

author: jtraglia
content: For 2, you could split the big batch into smaller sub-batches.

author: nflaig
content: what you mean by at the end? you can fill up `pending_deposits` over multiple epochs make it huge in theory, all for the same pubkey but invalid signatures, then you need 1 builder deposit for this pubkey to trigger a signature verification for all these new pending deposits, so if clients don't pre-verify here, it's over, because it could be up to 100k, depending how much ETH you wanna burn

author: nflaig
content: I don't know how to solve (4) you need to verify 8191 signatures one-by-one if they all invalid

author: jtraglia
content: Okay I see. That 1 builder deposit is the trigger.

author: nflaig
content: how did you test (2)?

author: nflaig
content: I think sprinkeling in 4-5 invalids in the whole 8k batch is enough to make most strategies very inefficient, what do clients do if there is a single invalid one?

author: nflaig
content: divide and conquer can be dos if you have more than 4-5 invalid sigs in the right place and very inefficient, so doing 1-by-1 might be better probably?
